text:
{
  "title": "Campus Lifestyle Archetype Margins",
  "group_name": "campus_lifestyle_archetype_margins",
  "family": "behavioral_archetype_affinity",
  "summary": "Represent each student by deterministic affinities to whole campus-lifestyle archetypes, capturing whether the overall pattern looks consistently healthy, consistently depleted, active-but-overstrained, inactive-but-otherwise-low-risk, or mixed.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Create bounded domain scores on [-1, 1] from raw columns only: rest = mean of sleep-duration score and sleep-quality score, with 7-9 hours = 1, 6-<7 or 9-<10 = 0.4, 5-<6 = 0, <5 or >=10 = -1, and poor/average/good = -1/0/1; activity = mean of step, exercise, calorie-expenditure, and reported-activity scores, with steps <5000/5000-9999/>=10000 = -1/0/1, exercise <20/20-59/>=60 minutes = -1/0/1, calories <1800/1800-2599/>=2600 = -1/0/1, and sedentary/moderate/active = -1/0/1; pressure = low/medium/high stress as 1/0/-1; substance = no/occasional/yes as 1/0/-1; nourishment = mean of diet balanced/veg/non-veg as 1/0.5/0 and water <1.5/1.5-<2/2-4/>4 liters as -1/0/1/0; physiology = mean of BMI 18.5-25/16-<18.5 or 25-<30/>=30 as 1/0/-1 and heart rate <=70/70-85/>85 as 1/0/-1. Compare the six-domain vector to prototypes balanced_fit=(1,1,1,1,1,1), depleted_multirisk=(-1,-1,-1,-1,-1,-1), active_overstrained=(-1,1,-1,0,0,-1), low_risk_inactive=(0,-1,1,1,0,1), and moderate_mixed=(0,0,0,0,0,0); for each prototype output affinity = mean over observed domains of clip(1 - abs(score - prototype) / 2, 0, 1), plus best archetype, best affinity, best-minus-second affinity margin, balanced-minus-multirisk margin, and active-overstrained-minus-balanced margin. Missing subcomponents are ignored within each domain, unknown categories are treated as missing, missing domains are omitted from prototype affinity calculations, and rows with no observed domains receive neutral affinities of 0.5. Source grounding: AHA Life's Essential 8 health-component framing at https://www.heart.org/en/healthy-living/healthy-lifestyle/lifes-essential-8 and university lifestyle-clustering evidence at https://www.mdpi.com/1660-4601/17/5/1776.",
  "expected_signal": "The target is an overall three-class health state, so affinities to coherent lifestyle profiles may expose interactional boundaries where fit rows are consistently balanced, unhealthy rows accumulate multidomain adverse signals, and at-risk rows often appear as mixed or compensated patterns rather than simple univariate extremes.",
  "risk": "Hand-coded archetypes may be redundant with raw features and prior lifestyle-score groups, thresholds from general health literature may not match the synthetic label generator, and neutral handling of missing values can dilute signal when missingness itself is informative."
}
